Items.Key: race condition in threadpool::enqueue()|race condition|cwe-362|high|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|500|515|threadpool::threadpool()|threadpool(size_t num_threads = config::performance::thread_pool_size) : stop_flag_(false) { for (size_t i = 0; i < num_threads; ++i) { workers_.emplace_back([this] {|concurrent access to task_queue_ without proper synchronization could lead to race conditions and undefined behavior|when multiple threads try to enqueue tasks simultaneously while stop_flag_ is being modified|ensure proper synchronization of all shared state. add mutex protection around task queue operations in threadpool class->race condition in threadpool::enqueue()|race condition|cwe-362|high|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|500|515|threadpool::threadpool()|threadpool(size_t num_threads = config::performance::thread_pool_size) : stop_flag_(false) { for (size_t i = 0; i < num_threads; ++i) { workers_.emplace_back([this] {|concurrent access to task_queue_ without proper synchronization could lead to race conditions and undefined behavior|when multiple threads try to enqueue tasks simultaneously while stop_flag_ is being modified|ensure proper synchronization of all shared state. add mutex protection around task queue operations in threadpool class

uninitialized memory in astnode constructor|use of uninitialized memory|cwe-908|medium|0,85|enhanced_calc.cpp|344|351|astnode constructor|struct astnode { math_engine::tokentype type; double value; std::string identifier; std::unique_ptr<astnode> left; std::unique_ptr<astnode> right; std::vector<std::unique_ptr<astnode>> children; astnode(math_engine::tokentype t = math_engine::tokentype::number) : type(t), value(0.0) {}|uninitialized memory could lead to undefined behavior when accessed later in the program|when astnode objects are created without proper initialization of all members|initialize all members in the constructor. change line 344 to: astnode(math_engine::tokentype t = math_engine::tokentype::number) : type(t), value(0.0), identifier(""), left(nullptr), right(nullptr) {}->uninitialized memory in astnode constructor|use of uninitialized memory|cwe-908|medium|0,85|enhanced_calc.cpp|344|351|astnode constructor|struct astnode { math_engine::tokentype type; double value; std::string identifier; std::unique_ptr<astnode> left; std::unique_ptr<astnode> right; std::vector<std::unique_ptr<astnode>> children; astnode(math_engine::tokentype t = math_engine::tokentype::number) : type(t), value(0.0) {}|uninitialized memory could lead to undefined behavior when accessed later in the program|when astnode objects are created without proper initialization of all members|initialize all members in the constructor. change line 344 to: astnode(math_engine::tokentype t = math_engine::tokentype::number) : type(t), value(0.0), identifier(""), left(nullptr), right(nullptr) {}

missing bounds check in stringtokenizer::tokenize()|buffer overflow|cwe-125|medium|0,85|enhanced_calc.cpp|248|257|stringtokenizer::tokenize()|while (start < input_.length()) { size_t end = input_.find_first_of(delimiters, start); if (end == std::string::npos) end = input_.length(); if (start < end) tokens_.push_back(input_.substr(start, end - start)); start = end + 1;|potential buffer overflow if the input string contains malformed tokens that exceed expected bounds|when processing very long or malformed input strings|add bounds checking for token extraction. change line 254 to: tokens_.push_back(input_.substr(start, std::min(end - start, config::limits::max_expression_length)));

Items.Notes: The previous output correctly identified the format string vulnerability but incorrectly labeled it as 'Format String Vulnerability' instead of 'Format-String Injection'. The evidence provided was accurate, but the title and function name were not precise enough.
The previous output did not contain this vulnerability. The function `fact` is present, but the integer overflow issue was not mentioned in the provided source text.
The previous output did not contain this vulnerability. The `cleanup` function is present, but the use-after-free issue was not mentioned in the provided source text.
The previous output did not contain this vulnerability. The `validate_config` function is present, but the command injection issue was not mentioned in the provided source text.
The previous output correctly identified the buffer overflow vulnerability in `safe_string_copy()` with accurate evidence and fix.
The previous output correctly identified the hardcoded admin secret in `config::security::ADMIN_SECRET` with accurate evidence and fix.
The previous output correctly identified the command injection vulnerability in `ConfigLoader::load_config()` with accurate evidence and fix.
The previous output correctly identified the command injection vulnerability in `AdminConsole::execute_admin_command()` with accurate evidence and fix.
The previous output correctly identified the path traversal vulnerability in `ConfigLoader::load_config()` with accurate evidence and fix.
The previous output correctly identified the persistent authentication state vulnerability in `AdminConsole::authenticate()` with accurate evidence and fix.
The previous output did not contain this vulnerability. The `handle_variable_command` function is present, but the unhandled exception issue was not mentioned in the provided source text.
The previous output correctly identified the integer underflow vulnerability in `MemoryPool::allocate()` with accurate evidence and fix.
The previous output correctly identified the recursion issue in `ExpressionParser::parse_expression()` with accurate evidence and fix.
The previous output correctly identified the data race vulnerability in `ExpressionEvaluator::evaluate_cached()` with accurate evidence and fix.
The previous output correctly identified the TOCTOU vulnerability in `ConfigLoader::load_config()` with accurate evidence and fix.
